clinical_information.diagnosis: Philadelphia chromosome-negative B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL), minimal residual disease positive (MRD+)
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Induction (4/1/24–4/29/24): vincristine (weekly), dexamethasone (daily x28 days), L-asparaginase (alternate days), daunorubicin (single dose wk1). Consolidation (5/6/24–7/1/24): high‑dose methotrexate (weekly), mercaptopurine (daily), vincristine (biweekly), cyclophosphamide (week 3), doxorubicin (final week). Maintenance started 7/8/24: methotrexate (weekly), mercaptopurine (daily), vincristine (every 4 weeks), intrathecal methotrexate (first dose 10/4/24). Bone marrow 3/29/24: 85% lymphoblasts at diagnosis. Repeat bone marrow 10/4/24: morphological remission with <5% blasts but persistent MRD by flow cytometry at 0.1% leukemic cells.
clinical_information.lab_summary: CBC: overall within reference ranges except for mild basophilia (2%, reference 0–1%). Bone marrow: 2024-03-29 marrow was hypercellular (~90%) with 85% lymphoblasts confirming B‑cell precursor ALL (Philadelphia chromosome–negative). 2024-10-04 marrow remains hypercellular (~90%) but shows morphologic remission with blasts <5%; however minimal residual disease (MRD) is positive with 0.1% leukemic cells. Interpretation: patient achieved morphological remission but has persistent MRD, which represents residual B-ALL and an increased risk of relapse and typically warrants closer monitoring and consideration of additional or alternative therapy despite otherwise largely normal peripheral counts.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient is overall clinically well with only mild fatigue and decreased energy reported by parents; afebrile and well appearing with normal vital signs and no other systemic symptoms reported.
clinical_information.prognosis_and_risk_if_not_approved: Persistent MRD (0.1%) after consolidation confers a high risk of relapse and poorer long-term outcomes. Without timely MRD-directed therapy (e.g., blinatumomab), the patient is at increased risk of progression to overt leukemic relapse, likely need for more intensive salvage treatment and/or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, and increased morbidity and mortality.
clinical_information.clinical_rationale_for_urgency: MRD positivity after consolidation indicates residual disease despite standard therapy and is time-sensitive; prompt eradication of MRD is indicated to reduce relapse risk. Blinatumomab is an approved MRD-directed therapy for pediatric B-ALL and early initiation is clinically indicated to attempt MRD clearance and prevent progression to overt relapse.

treatment_request.duration: Induction cycle 1: Days 1-28: 28 mcg/day; Duration of therapy: Based on clinical response
treatment_request.rationale: Treatment for B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL). Patient has received 1 standard induction and consolidation cycle and is now in maintenance phase. See attached clinical documentation, laboratory results and pathology reports.

(category 2A) TKIs for Ph+ ALL in adults and adolescents/young adults include Sprycel,

B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in adults 
and children.  
 
Policy/Criteria 
Provider must submit documentation (such as office chart notes, lab results or other clinical information) 
supporting that member has met all approval criteria.  
 
It is the policy of health plans affiliated with Envolve Pharmacy Solutions™ that Blincyto is  medically 
necessary when the following criteria are met:  
 I. Initial Approval Criteria  
A. Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (must meet all) : 
1. Diagnosis of B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B -ALL);  
2. Prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist  or hematologist ; 
3. Requested as treatment for (a or b):  
a. B-ALL in remission but positive for minimal residual disease (MRD+);  
b. Relapsed or refractory B -ALL (i and ii):  
i. Philadelphia chromosome- negative (Ph- ) disease;  
ii. Philadelphia chromosome- positive (Ph+) disease and intolerant or refractory to at 
least one second- generation or later tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI; i.e., Sprycel
®, 
Tasigna®, Bosulif®, Iclusig®); 
*Prior authorization is (or may be) required for these agents  
4. Dose does  not exceed 28 mcg/day.  
Approval duration:

Page 1 of 4 
Clinical Policy: Blinatumomab (Blincyto)  
Reference N umber: ERX.SPA. 241 
Effective Date: 09 .01.18 
Last Review Date: 08.18  Revision Log  
 
See Important Reminder  at the end of this policy for important regulatory and legal information.  
 
Description 
Blinatumomab (Blincyto®) is a bispecific CD19- directed CD3 T -cell engager that binds to CD19  
(expressed on cells of B -lineage origin) and CD3 (expressed on T cells).  
 
FDA Approved Indication(s) 
Blincyto is indicated for :  
• MRD -positive B -cell precursor ALL  
o Treatment of B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in first or second complete 
remission with minimal residual disease (MRD) greater than or equal to 0.1% in adults and 
children. This indication is approved under accelerated approval based on MRD response rate and hematological relapse- free survival. Continued approval for this indication may be contingent 
upon verification and description of clinical benefit in the confirmatory trials.  
• Relapsed or refractory B -cell precursor ALL  
o Treatment of relapsed or refractory B -cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) in adults 
and children.  
 
Policy/Criteria

Reviews,

decision.reason: The request meets all policy initial approval criteria: diagnosis is B-cell precursor ALL in remission with MRD positive (0.1%), prescribed by a pediatric hematology/oncology specialist, requested as treatment for MRD+ B-ALL, and the requested dose (28 mcg/day) does not exceed the policy maximum. These facts align with the policy indication for Blincyto for MRD-positive B-ALL (≥0.1%) and the dosing regimen for induction (Days 1-28: 28 mcg/day).
